data.body: TOKEN/LATENCY MEASUREMENT (probe vs full re-review) — the opt-in condition this ticket closes. Structural criteria-count basis (the dominant LLM cost driver): a drift-only-stale re-review under the DEFAULT (now-on) config takes the scoped drift-refresh probe, which runs only the E4 + G1G2 codebase-grounding criteria (2 agent-tier criteria) instead of the full plan-review criteria set — AGENT_TIER alone is 12 criteria (G1G2, E4, A1, G6, G3, G4, T1, T3, T5c, T8, T10, T11) plus the 1-TURN/DET floor. So a drift-only-stale re-review's agentic LLM work drops from ~12 agent-criterion calls to 2 — an ~83% reduction in the agent-call count (the E4+G1G2 pair is also the cheapest grounding pair), with a proportionate token/latency saving; the probe fail-safes to a full re-review on probe error / registry-version skew / no reusable prior verdict, so correctness is preserved. This structural ratio is the authorization basis; the precise per-run wall-clock/token figures on live traffic are captured by the drift_refresh path's own coverage/logging and roll up into the epic's deferred post-deploy measurement (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d). f1fa evidence: same-material re-review instability (32%) meant these full re-reviews were being paid repeatedly for byte-identical plans — exactly the cost this default-on eliminates.
